---
name: do
description: 'Manifest executor. Works through Deliverables verifying every Acceptance Criterion and Global Invariant. Use when executing a manifest, running a plan, implementing a defined task. Triggers: do, execute, run the manifest, implement plan, run plan, execute manifest, ship the manifest.'
argument-hint: '<manifest-path>'
user-invocable: true
---

Work toward the manifest's Deliverables. Before calling `/done`, verify every Acceptance Criterion and Global Invariant by spawning one subagent per criterion using its `verify.prompt:` verbatim — no rewording. (Multi-repo manifests declaring `Repos:` prepend the path map per `define/references/MULTI_REPO.md`; otherwise nothing wraps the author's prompt.) The optional `verify.agent:` names the subagent type (default: general-purpose); the optional `verify.model:` selects the model. Respect `phase:` ordering — serial across phases, parallel within. Each verifier returns PASS, FAIL, or BLOCKED; all must PASS before `/done`. Any BLOCKED routes via `/escalate`.

**FAIL body contract.** FAIL bodies carry per-gate **findings** (specialized verifiers like `github-pr-lifecycle`) or a natural-language hint over the whole body (generic verifiers). Per-gate findings come in two shapes — inline (`FAIL — <suggested>`) or multi-line with named `Reason:` / `Suggested:` / `Context:` fields. The `Suggested:` field carries one of two things: (1) a vocabulary token from the verifier's fixed workflow-neutral set — `/do` executes the token literally, verbatim, no substitution; or (2) free-form prose describing a solvable-but-novel situation — `/do` reads with LLM judgment and decides the next step. Workflow decisions — including `/escalate` routing — live caller-side; the agent reports findings, `/do` decides. Multi-line `Reason:` and `Context:` fields are diagnostic. Prose hints from generic verifiers are also read with LLM judgment.

**Substitution prevention** (literal-execution discipline this skill owns per ADR `20260518-verifier-fail-hints-are-directives`). For `bash sleep <N>; reinvoke` directives in particular: **do NOT substitute `Stop`, `/loop`, `ScheduleWakeup`, or busy-waiting (re-invoking the verifier without the intervening sleep) for the sleep. Those four substitutions bypass the polling contract and the wait silently dies — exactly the failure modes observed in the wild that this rule prevents.** The rule generalizes to all vocabulary tokens: execute the literal command; don't paraphrase, substitute, or invent an alternative mechanism the model might think is "equivalent."

**Iteration + routing.** Code-change fix attempts iterate until they pass or `/do` judges them genuinely unrecoverable → `/escalate`. Other retry shapes (waiting, retriggering, replying with or without resolving, mechanical syncs) execute the verifier's vocabulary tokens until the next reinvocation reports PASS or until a finding's prose surfaces a situation `/do` judges human-decision-needed. When a hint or prose finding indicates a reviewer ask beyond the work's intent, route to amendment via `manifest-dev:define --amend`. **When a hint or prose finding indicates terminal / unrecoverable / human-decision-needed, route to `/escalate` — autonomously amending the manifest to suppress the block is forbidden.**

Mid-/do user messages default to invoking `manifest-dev:define` for amendment — the manifest is the source of truth, silent scope drift is worse than an extra amendment cycle. Pure questions about the manifest or process are answered inline.

**Input.** `<manifest-path>` — required; no args → halt with usage. Read the manifest fully before any execution. Multi-repo manifests (declare `Repos: [name: path, ...]` in Intent) — use absolute paths in tool calls when working in a non-cwd repo.
